open_known_gaps.summary: Client-side memory-exhaustion (PDF-bomb DoS) class — tracked, no server impact (a user can only exhaust their own browser tab). The viewer.js loadDocument() LOAD boundary still has no numPages>1000 / cumulative-byte cap and no processing timeout: after getDocument(...).promise it calls renderAll() (TASK-316 supersede-race guard present, so footprint is linear, one canvas per page, not multiplied). All per-page / whole-doc consumers sit downstream of this one uncapped boundary: (1) viewer.js renderAll() one canvas/page; (2) thumbnails.js a 2nd canvas/page (mitigated by 120px width, dpr cap 2, sequential render, renderToken guard); (3) merge.js mergeAndDownload() loads base.getData()+each queued file with no cumulative byte/page cap; (4) pages.js downloadRotated() and page-numbers.js applyPageNumbers() getData()->pdf-lib.load()->save() over the open doc; (5) NEW this tick: metadata.js (TASK-330) applyMetadata() same getData()->load()->save() pattern, and convert.js (TASK-331) renderPageToImage() renders ONE page to a throwaway canvas with its OWN MAX=8000px dimension cap. Items 2-5 are NOT new load boundaries (they operate on the already-validated open document). Single right fix = developer task: a page-count/byte cap at the viewer.js LOAD boundary protects every downstream consumer at once. Documented in docs/security-guide.md.

audit_notes.innerHTML_in_js: only empty-string clears (container/list/layer .innerHTML='') or comments — no user/PDF content sink anywhere in the JS tree
audit_notes.inline_scripts_index_html: none — no <script>inline</script>, no on*= handlers, no javascript: URIs; only 2 same-origin script tags (lib/pdf-lib.min.js defer, js/app.js module) — CSP-safe
audit_notes.external_fetch_or_xhr: none — no fetch/XMLHttpRequest/sendBeacon/WebSocket and no non-local http(s) URLs anywhere in js/ (100% client-side, PDFs never leave the browser)
audit_notes.convert_js: TASK-331 export page to PNG/JPEG. Renders ONE page of the open doc to a throwaway canvas (never the viewer canvas) with MAX=8000px/side dimension cap (built-in memory guard). buildFileName() strips to [a-zA-Z0-9._-], slice(0,180), default 'document' -> <base>_page-<n>.<ext>. Safe Blob(image/*)+createObjectURL+a.download+rel=noopener download, anchor removed, URL revoked after 1s. Page number clamped 1..numPages. status via textContent. Operates on already-open doc via getPage(); no new load boundary.
audit_notes.metadata_js: TASK-330 view+edit Title/Author/Subject/Keywords. Read-only props rendered via addRow()->textContent into a <dl>; el.innerHTML only used to clear. Editable values via .value, written through pdf-lib setTitle/setAuthor/setSubject/setKeywords (never the DOM). buildFileName() same sanitizer -> <base>_metadata.pdf. Same safe download pattern. parsePdfDate uses a numeric regex, no injection. Operates on already-open doc via getData(); no new load boundary.
audit_notes.secrets_scan_hit: all false positives — ssh-login-detector.sh grep patterns, 'token'/'keywords' variable + feature names, and status.json mentioning the 'git_secrets' check itself; no real secret
